ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #28, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Nov-18-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Stepehn Gould and Kathleen Michon appeared by zoom for Sue O'Keeffe (Trustee).

The Court stated that it reviewed the response to the Probate Examiner's notes, that it provides information explaining why the work that was done took that amount of time, but the issue really is why the work was necessary and relevant in any event, and noted that they already charged 3.55 hours for reviewing the accounting. Mr. Gould stated that the trust was created close to thirty years ago, provided history, stated that they simplified and minimized expenses by filing one single accounting for all three trusts, that different Probate Examiners develop different questions regarding the accountings and that having to report to Court is unusual for them. Mr. Gould also stated that the Examiner found an error/discrepancy in the 26th account, that there was an error in allocation regarding a small amount of activity, that when the error was found they had to go back to the account preparer and carefully review the accounting, and that the work had to be done in order to satisfy the Examiner and the Court, and to get the accounting settled. The Court stated that an additional 7.5 hours for reviewing the accounting is concerning. The 28th Account and Report of Successor Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Jaime. Not reported.
